summary: Excellent soundboard recording of a historic February ’68 show with a flawless Anthem of the Sun second set and standout performances of Dark Star, The Eleven, Cryptical>Other One and more.
review: The show is hailed as the pinnacle of the February run, especially the second‑set rendition of the entire *Anthem of the Sun* album, which the reviewer calls “nearly perfect.”  The soundboard source is praised for its crystal‑clear mix and balanced instruments, making every tight transition and inspired jam—from Dark Star to Caution—readily audible.  Overall the performance is described as energetic, smooth, and full of memorable moments.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed five‑star review praises both musical performance and recording quality; the second review provides no substantive rating, so confidence is moderate.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewer notes excellent audio and mixing, crisp and clean sound throughout with no reported drop‑outs or balance issues.

standout_songs: Anthem of the Sun (full second set)
show_quality.setlist_flow: Transitions are described as smooth and flawless, giving the concert an effortless flow from the first set into a masterful second set.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for being "in the zone" on Dark Star, delivering perfect tone, and skillfully guiding transitions (e.g., New Potato).
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for artful improvisation after vocals on New Potato and strong organ work that drives The Eleven.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen’s organ noted as a driving force on The Eleven; overall keyboard work described as solid throughout the show.
